user_query:
Please read the memory folder under input/memory/ and perform a consolidation plus an index build, then write all results under output/ as follows:

1) Anchor promotion and log compression
- Read input/memory/ANCHORS.md and the daily logs input/memory/2026-04-12.md and input/memory/2026-04-13.md.
- From each daily log, extract any lines that contain "[ANCHOR]" and take the remainder of the line with the tag removed and whitespace trimmed. For each extracted anchor, append to output/memory/ANCHORS.md using exactly this format (one block per anchor):
  "### YYYY-MM-DD | Auto-promoted | <anchor text>\nStatus: Active\n"
  where YYYY-MM-DD is the date in the log filename. Do not append a duplicate if the first 40 characters of the anchor text already appear anywhere in the anchors file content.
- Also, compress the daily logs by collapsing runs of multiple consecutive blank lines into a single blank line (content lines unchanged). Save the compressed logs to output/memory/2026-04-12.md and output/memory/2026-04-13.md.

2) Schema chunk index (no embeddings)
- Read all schema files under input/memory/schemas/ (specifically: project-demo.md and personal-health.md).
- Chunk the text using these exact rules:
  - CHUNK_SIZE = 400 characters, CHUNK_OVERLAP = 80 characters.
  - Build chunks by accumulating lines; count per line as len(line) + 1 (for the newline). When the accumulated count is >= CHUNK_SIZE, emit a chunk, then start the next chunk with the trailing overlap measured by the same counting.
  - Track a section name as the most recent heading line that starts with "## " or "### "; the section is the heading with all leading "#" removed and then stripped of whitespace. If no heading seen yet, the section is "general".
  - For each chunk, compute chunk_id as the first 8 hex characters of the MD5 of the chunk text.
  - Set source to the path of the schema file relative to the memory folder, e.g., "schemas/project-demo.md".
- Save all chunks in output/vector_index.json as a JSON array of objects with exactly these keys per chunk: {"text", "source", "section", "chunk_id"}.
- Also write output/index_meta.json with: {"chunk_count": <int>, "files": ["schemas/personal-health.md", "schemas/project-demo.md"] sorted ascending}.

3) Summary report
- Write output/report.json with: {"anchors_promoted": <int>, "logs_processed": 2, "chunk_count": <int>, "files": ["schemas/personal-health.md", "schemas/project-demo.md"]} where counts align with what you produced above.

Do not modify files under input/. Only write the outputs under output/ at the exact paths described.
